name: Write Lattice Data
description: Writes a value to a writable lattice path. Accepted namespaces: 'w/' (your workspace), 'o/' (your operation pins), 'n/' (an agent's own workspace, agent-scoped only), and 't/' (job-scoped temp, job-scoped only). Writes to framework-managed namespaces ('g/' agents, 's/' secrets, 'j/' jobs) are not permitted — use the corresponding agent:*, secret:*, or job APIs instead. Writes to 'v/' (venue globals) are rejected unless the caller is the venue identity. Path is '/'-separated; intermediate maps are created on demand. The value can be any JSON-compatible type; writing null removes the entry. Returns {written: true} on success.

path.description: Writable lattice path. Must start with one of: 'w/' (workspace), 'o/' (operation pins), 'n/' (agent scope), 't/' (job temp). Deep paths create intermediate maps.

value.description: The value to store. Use structured values (objects/arrays/scalars) rather than serialised strings. Null removes the entry.

written.description: True if the value was written successfully
